.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2773:   833	      // REQ-034 (042 closing audit, F015): surface non-P0 release-readiness
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2870:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-006.md:19:- **F012**: Improve-agent docs promise resumable lineage modes that the shipped workflow cannot execute or surface — `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:292` — The improvement skill says sessions support `new`, `resume`, `restart`, `fork`, and `completed-continue`, and that resume replays prior artifacts to compute `continuedFromIteration` before dispatch (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/SKILL.md:292-294`); the operator-facing improve command repeats that contract and says `--session-id=<prior-id>` resumes from the saved journal without re-running completed iterations (`.opencode/command/improve/agent.md:332-339`). But the shipped auto/confirm workflows only accept `target_path`, `target_profile`, `scoring_mode`, `spec_folder`, `max_iterations`, and execution mode as inputs (`.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:36-42`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_confirm.yaml:37-43`), and their runtime only emits `session_start`, per-iteration, and `session_end` journal events with no lineage-branch input or transition step (`.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_auto.yaml:132-183`, `.opencode/command/improve/assets/improve_agent-improver_confirm.yaml:148-217`). The reducer then summarizes only the latest session timestamps plus `stopReason` / `sessionOutcome` and never surfaces session ancestry, lineage mode, or `continuedFromIteration` (`.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:184-224`, `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:781-817`). That leaves the improvement bundle advertising safe resume/restart/fork/reopen behavior that operators cannot actually invoke or audit in the live runtime.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2872:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-006.md:40:- Candidate-lineage depth as proof of session lifecycle support: `candidate-lineage.json` only summarizes candidate ancestry within a run, so it cannot substantiate resume/restart/fork/completed-continue session semantics — `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:227-267`.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2873:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:4:This pass targeted lifecycle/session metadata across the research and review runtime mirrors, with emphasis on the user-visible `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` branches and the JSONL lineage records they are supposed to persist. I compared the active YAML entrypoints against the state-format, loop-protocol, and quick-reference docs to check whether restart/reopen behavior is actually encoded or only described.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2875:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:22:- **F011**: Resume-event examples remain skeletal even where the visible state contract expects lineage metadata — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` — The research loop protocol still shows `{"type":"event","event":"resumed","fromIteration":N}` at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:83` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md:142`, while the review loop protocol only upgrades that example to `sessionId` / `generation` / `lineageMode` but still omits `timestamp` and the continuation fields at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md:519-522`. The state-format references describe lifecycle events as first-class JSONL rows keyed by lineage metadata (`sessionId`, `continuedFromRun`, `timestamp`, and restart/fork ancestry fields) at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/state_format.md:240-243` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/state_format.md:240-243`, and the quick references market these branches as live surfaces at `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/references/quick_reference.md:87-90` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/references/quick_reference.md:83`. Even if F010 is fixed later, these examples would still train operators toward under-specified lineage rows.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2877:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-005.md:34:- Novelty justification: F010 is the first runtime-backed finding in this session that ties the exposed lifecycle branch surface to missing lineage persistence across both research and review YAMLs. F011 is narrower and document-only, but it is also new: prior iterations did not isolate the resume-event examples themselves as a separate contract-drift surface.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2881:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-007.md:42:- Lifecycle transition persistence via reducer summaries: The reducer/test surfaces expose `lineageMode`, `generation`, and `sessionId`, but the inspected paths did not reveal any persisted `resumed`, `restarted`, `forked`, or `completed_continue` event handling.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2890:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/review-report.md:141:- **Required decision**: either implement `resume` / `restart` / `fork` / `completed-continue` as real runtime branches with event emission and ancestry metadata or narrow every user-facing promise to match the current one-session model.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2905:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-004.md:37:- Reducer-owned graph/session dashboard fields: No new drift surfaced here because the reducer still exposes `sessionId`, `generation`, `lineageMode`, `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, and `graphBlockers` consistently — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:522-538` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:742-805`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2906:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-004.md:45:Rotate into the remaining lifecycle/session metadata and completed-continue/reopen mirrors across review and research docs, especially places where resume/restart/fork examples may still lag the persisted JSONL and config contracts.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2937:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/tests/fixtures/interrupted-session/research/deep-research-strategy.md:85:- Capability matrix: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/assets/runtime_capabilities.json`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2938:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/tests/fixtures/interrupted-session/research/deep-research-strategy.md:87:- Capability resolver: `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/runtime-capabilities.cjs`
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2949:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:140:          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2951:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:143:          ask: "Existing research session is consistent. Resume where it left off, or restart (archive the current research/ tree and start a fresh session)?"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2955:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:149:            append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"research","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2959:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:157:            append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"research","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{spec_folder}/research_archive/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2960:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:162:          note: "fork and completed-continue branches are deferred. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are runtime-supported in this release. See sk-deep-research/references/loop_protocol.md `Session Lifecycle` for the canonical one-session model."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2965:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_confirm.yaml:280:          log_event: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"userPaused","sentinelPath":"{spec_folder}/research/.deep-research-pause","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2977:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:33:  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:2979:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:164:          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"

Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3144:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_confirm.yaml:164:          - session_state: "fresh | resume | completed-session | invalid-state"

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3276:.opencode/skill/sk-deep-research/scripts/tests/fixtures/interrupted-session/README.md:1:This fixture simulates a deep-research run that crashed while writing iteration 3, leaving both a malformed `research/deep-research-state.jsonl` tail record and a partially written `research/iterations/iteration-003.md`. A fail-closed reducer should preserve iterations 1-2 as durable evidence, flag or reject the corrupted run-3 state in strict mode, and allow explicit lenient recovery to continue with a partial registry plus resume-oriented next steps. For Gate E continuity, these fixture artifacts remain supporting evidence only; broader packet recovery still routes through `/spec_kit:resume`, then `handover.md`, `_memory.continuity`, and the remaining spec docs.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3666:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml:353:          append_to_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"stuckRecovery","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"stuckRecovery","fromIteration":{current_iteration},"outcome":"recovered","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3667:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml:365:          canonical_event: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"userPaused","sentinelPath":"{spec_folder}/research/.deep-research-pause","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3668:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-research_auto.yaml:368:          canonical_event: '{"type":"event","event":"stuckRecovery","mode":"research","run":{current_iteration},"stopReason":"stuckRecovery","fromIteration":{current_iteration},"outcome":"recovered","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{config.lineage.sessionId}","generation":{config.lineage.generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3669:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:33:  lineage_mode: "[auto|resume|restart] - Session lifecycle intent. Default: auto. The runtime only persists lineage events for `resume` (same sessionId, no archive) and `restart` (new sessionId, generation+1, prior review/ archived). `fork` and `completed-continue` are deferred and no longer accepted — see sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3670:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:166:          log: "Auto-resume: state artifacts are complete and consistent. Continue the same lineage (sessionId unchanged, generation unchanged, no archive)."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3671:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:167:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3672:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:170:          action: "Archive the current review/ tree under review_archive/{timestamp}/ and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3674:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:176:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"restarted","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{prior_session_id}","lineageMode":"restart","generation":{generation},"archivedPath":"{spec_folder}/review_archive/{timestamp_slug}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3675:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:178:        note_deferred_branches: "fork and completed-continue are deferred and no longer runtime-supported in this release. The reducer still reads `lineageMode` from config for dashboards and analysis, but only `new`, `resume`, and `restart` are live. See sk-deep-review/references/loop_protocol.md §Lifecycle Branches for the one-session contract."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3680:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:274:        content: '{"type":"config","mode":"review","reviewTarget":"{review_target}","reviewTargetType":"{review_target_type}","reviewDimensions":{review_dimensions_json},"sessionId":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","parentSessionId":null,"lineageMode":"new","generation":1,"continuedFromRun":null,"maxIterations":{max_iterations},"convergenceThreshold":{convergence_threshold},"createdAt":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","specFolder":"{spec_folder}"}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3681:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:279:        content: '{"sessionId":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","generation":1,"lineageMode":"new","openFindings":[],"resolvedFindings":[],"repeatedFindings":[],"dimensionCoverage":{"correctness":false,"security":false,"traceability":false,"maintainability":false},"findingsBySeverity":{"P0":0,"P1":0,"P2":0},"openFindingsCount":0,"resolvedFindingsCount":0,"convergenceScore":0.0}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3683:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:370:        append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"graph_convergence","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"decision":"{graph_decision}","signals":{graph_signals_json},"blockers":{graph_blockers_json},"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3684:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:486:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"blocked_stop","mode":"review","run":{current_iteration},"blockedBy":{blocked_by_json},"gateResults":{"convergenceGate":{"pass":{convergence_gate_pass},"score":{convergence_gate_score}},"dimensionCoverageGate":{"pass":{dimension_coverage_gate_pass},"covered":{dimension_coverage_gate_covered_json},"missing":{dimension_coverage_gate_missing_json}},"p0ResolutionGate":{"pass":{p0_resolution_gate_pass},"activeP0":{active_p0_count}},"evidenceDensityGate":{"pass":{evidence_density_gate_pass},"avgEvidencePerFinding":{avg_evidence_per_finding}},"hotspotSaturationGate":{"pass":{hotspot_saturation_gate_pass}},"claimAdjudicationGate":{"pass":{claim_adjudication_gate_pass},"activeP0P1":{claim_adjudication_active_count}}},"recoveryStrategy":"{recovery_strategy}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3685:.opencode/command/spec_kit/assets/spec_kit_deep-review_auto.yaml:517:          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"userPaused","mode":"review","stopReason":"userPaused","reason":"{pending_stop_detail}","timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}","sessionId":"{session_id}","generation":{generation}}'

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:3985:   300	- **Focus Alignment**: Compare current key questions against `config.originalKeyQuestions` (the snapshot of initial key questions populated at session init). Flag any answer outside the declared scope, or any significant drift where the current question set no longer resembles the original set. When `originalKeyQuestions` is absent (legacy packets), fall back to comparing against strategy.md's initialized question list.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4647:Title: Research reducer drops terminal stop metadata and reports stale session state  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4649:Evidence: `buildRegistry()` only lifts `blocked_stop` into `blockedStopHistory` and `buildGraphConvergenceRollup()` only looks at `graph_convergence` (`reduce-state.cjs:396-405`). `renderDashboard()` prints `config.status`, session, parent session, lifecycle mode, and generation, but no terminal reason (`reduce-state.cjs:563-570`). The real packet still records `{"event":"synthesis_complete","stopReason":"maxIterationsReached"}` at [deep-research-state.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-state.jsonl:22), while the generated dashboard status at [deep-research-dashboard.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-dashboard.md:17) still shows `INITIALIZED`.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4658:Description: The workflow now appends `resumed` and `restarted` events, but the reducer still reads lineage only from `config.lineage.*`. On resume, the workflow skips directly back into the loop after appending the event, so config never changes from `lineageMode:"new"`. On restart, it sets temporary variables, then recreates config with hardcoded `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, and `continuedFromRun:null`. The result is that resumed or restarted sessions will still be rendered as fresh runs, defeating the Phase 001 resume-boundary contract.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4669:Evidence: `buildBlockedStopHistory()` handles `blocked_stop`, and `buildRegistry()` stores only blocked-stop / graph-convergence-derived fields (`reduce-state.cjs:464-540`). `renderDashboard()` status also prints only config-derived lifecycle fields and verdict/counts (`reduce-state.cjs:734-744`). The archived 042 review session still persists `{"event":"synthesis_complete","stopReason":"maxIterationsReached"}` at [deep-review-state.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-state.jsonl:12). The shared optimizer corpus also shows a terminal review event with a nested `legalStop` bundle, but no reducer path reads it: [sample-040-corpus.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/fixtures/deep-loop-optimizer/sample-040-corpus.jsonl:6).  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4678:Description: Review mode has the same structural bug as research mode. The workflows append `resumed` and `restarted` JSONL events, but the reducer keeps reading lineage from config fields that remain hardcoded to fresh-session defaults. That means a resumed or restarted review can still be summarized as `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, `continuedFromRun:null`, even though the JSONL event log says otherwise.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4679:Evidence: Auto mode appends lifecycle events at `.yaml:167` and `.yaml:176`, but its config creation still writes `parentSessionId:null`, `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, `continuedFromRun:null` at `.yaml:241-244`. Confirm mode mirrors the same bug at `.yaml:173`, `.yaml:181`, and `.yaml:249-252`. The reducer only preserves `sessionId`, `generation`, and `lineageMode` from config in the registry (`reduce-state.cjs:521-524`) and only prints those same config fields in the dashboard (`reduce-state.cjs:742-744`).  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4705:Title: Research reducer drops terminal stop metadata and reports stale session state  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4707:Evidence: `buildRegistry()` only lifts `blocked_stop` into `blockedStopHistory` and `buildGraphConvergenceRollup()` only looks at `graph_convergence` (`reduce-state.cjs:396-405`). `renderDashboard()` prints `config.status`, session, parent session, lifecycle mode, and generation, but no terminal reason (`reduce-state.cjs:563-570`). The real packet still records `{"event":"synthesis_complete","stopReason":"maxIterationsReached"}` at [deep-research-state.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-state.jsonl:22), while the generated dashboard status at [deep-research-dashboard.md](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/research/deep-research-dashboard.md:17) still shows `INITIALIZED`.

On resume, the workflow skips directly back into the loop after appending the event, so config never changes from `lineageMode:"new"`. On restart, it sets temporary variables, then recreates config with hardcoded `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, and `continuedFromRun:null`. The result is that resumed or restarted sessions will still be rendered as fresh runs, defeating the Phase 001 resume-boundary contract.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4727:Evidence: `buildBlockedStopHistory()` handles `blocked_stop`, and `buildRegistry()` stores only blocked-stop / graph-convergence-derived fields (`reduce-state.cjs:464-540`). `renderDashboard()` status also prints only config-derived lifecycle fields and verdict/counts (`reduce-state.cjs:734-744`). The archived 042 review session still persists `{"event":"synthesis_complete","stopReason":"maxIterationsReached"}` at [deep-review-state.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/deep-review-state.jsonl:12). The shared optimizer corpus also shows a terminal review event with a nested `legalStop` bundle, but no reducer path reads it: [sample-040-corpus.jsonl](/Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public/.opencode/skill/system-spec-kit/scripts/tests/fixtures/deep-loop-optimizer/sample-040-corpus.jsonl:6).  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4736:Description: Review mode has the same structural bug as research mode. The workflows append `resumed` and `restarted` JSONL events, but the reducer keeps reading lineage from config fields that remain hardcoded to fresh-session defaults. That means a resumed or restarted review can still be summarized as `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, `continuedFromRun:null`, even though the JSONL event log says otherwise.  
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/iterations/iteration-001.md:4737:Evidence: Auto mode appends lifecycle events at `.yaml:167` and `.yaml:176`, but its config creation still writes `parentSessionId:null`, `lineageMode:"new"`, `generation:1`, `continuedFromRun:null` at `.yaml:241-244`. Confirm mode mirrors the same bug at `.yaml:173`, `.yaml:181`, and `.yaml:249-252`. The reducer only preserves `sessionId`, `generation`, and `lineageMode` from config in the registry (`reduce-state.cjs:521-524`) and only prints those same config fields in the dashboard (`reduce-state.cjs:742-744`).

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/review/archive-rvw-2026-04-11/iterations/iteration-004.md:37:- Reducer-owned graph/session dashboard fields: No new drift surfaced here because the reducer still exposes `sessionId`, `generation`, `lineageMode`, `graphConvergenceScore`, `graphDecision`, and `graphBlockers` consistently — `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:522-538` and `.opencode/skill/sk-deep-review/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:742-805`

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:407:The sk-improve-agent skill runs bounded improvement loops over agent packets, but it currently lacks the runtime truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 for deep-research and deep-review. This phase ports those contracts into agent-improver: a well-formed stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journaling, mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory analysis, trade-off detection, optional parallel candidate waves, and scoring weight optimization based on session history.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:409:**Key Decisions**: emit journal events in the orchestrator (not in the proposal-only agent); reuse the existing coverage graph infrastructure with an improvement-specific namespace; treat dimension trajectory as a first-class convergence signal; keep parallel candidate waves opt-in behind an exploration-breadth gate.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:426:| **Predecessor Phases** | `001-runtime-truth-foundation`, `002-semantic-coverage-graph`, `003-wave-executor`, `004-offline-loop-optimizer` |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:441:Apply the same runtime truth contracts to agent-improver so that every improvement session is auditable, resumable, convergence-aware, and self-improving over time.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:452:- Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all session events
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:460:- Scoring weight optimizer using historical session data
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:481:| `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md` | Modify | Add resume semantics, session-id propagation, weight optimizer invocation |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:482:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` | Create | Append-only JSONL event emitter for improvement session events |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:488:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/mutation-coverage.vitest.ts` | Create | Tests for coverage graph read/write and namespace isolation |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:503:| REQ-AI-001 | Stop-reason taxonomy: `converged`, `promoted`, `rolledBack`, `maxIterationsReached`, `regressionDetected`, `manualStop`, `error` | Every session termination emits exactly one stop-reason matching the taxonomy; no session ends without a logged stop-reason |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:504:| REQ-AI-002 | Legal-stop gates using 5-dimension stability + no regression + promotion criteria | A session may not claim `converged` or `promoted` unless all gate conditions pass; violations emit `regressionDetected` or continue the loop |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:505:| REQ-AI-003 | Resume/continuation semantics for improvement sessions | A session started with a prior session-id replays journal state and resumes from the last checkpoint without repeating completed iterations |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:506:| REQ-AI-004 | Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all events (iteration-started, candidate-proposed, candidate-evaluated, promotion-gate-checked, session-ended) | All five event types appear in the journal for every completed session; file is append-only and survives process restart |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:513:| REQ-AI-006 | Mutation coverage graph tracking explored dimensions, tried mutations, and integration surfaces using `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace | After each iteration the coverage graph is updated; querying it returns accurate explored/remaining sets per dimension |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:514:| REQ-AI-007 | Dimension trajectory tracking (per-dimension score history across iterations) | For each active session the trajectory file contains a time-ordered score vector per dimension; minimum 3 data points before convergence can be claimed |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:523:| REQ-AI-011 | Candidate lineage graph linking each candidate to its parent session, wave index, and spawning mutation | The lineage graph for any parallel-wave session can be traversed from root to leaf; each node contains session-id, wave-index, and mutation-type |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:529:| REQ-AI-012 | Scoring weight optimizer using historical session data to recommend per-dimension weight adjustments | After a configurable number of sessions the optimizer reads the journal history and emits a weight-recommendation report; recommendations do not auto-apply without explicit approval |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:530:| REQ-AI-013 | Benchmark replay stability measurement confirming consistent scores across identical replays | The benchmark-stability script reports a stability coefficient for each dimension; sessions with coefficient below threshold emit a `stabilityWarning` to the journal |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:538:- **SC-001**: Every improvement session termination produces a journal entry with exactly one stop-reason from the approved taxonomy.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:540:- **SC-003**: A session resumed from a prior session-id replays correctly and does not duplicate iteration events.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:544:- **SC-007**: The scoring weight optimizer produces a recommendation report after the configured session count threshold is reached.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:546:- **SC-009**: Backward compatibility is maintained: existing improvement sessions with no new config fields behave identically to pre-phase behavior.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:557:| Dependency | 042 Phase 2 coverage graph | Coverage graph namespace and API must support `loop_type: "improvement"` | Add namespace field to existing graph writers; verify no breaking change to deep-research/review paths |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:560:| Risk | Parallel wave complexity overloading LLM context | Parallel waves increase context length per session | Keep parallel waves strictly opt-in; default config disables them |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:591:- Resume of a session that previously ended with `error`: replay up to the last successful checkpoint, then continue.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:596:- Coverage graph file missing at session start: create a fresh graph; do not error.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:650:- [ ] 042 Phase 2 coverage graph API confirmed to support `loop_type` namespace parameter
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:659:- [ ] Backward compatibility verified: existing improvement sessions with no new config fields behave identically
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:675:- **improvement-journal.cjs**: Append-only JSONL emitter. Receives structured event objects from the orchestrator and appends them to the session journal file. Enforces the event schema and blocks invalid event types.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:676:- **mutation-coverage.cjs**: Coverage graph reader/writer. Tracks explored dimensions, tried mutation types per dimension, integration surfaces touched, and exhausted mutation sets. Uses `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace to isolate from deep-research/review paths.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:678:- **candidate-lineage.cjs**: Builds and queries a directed graph of candidate proposals across parallel wave sessions. Links each node to its parent session-id, wave index, and spawning mutation type.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:683:Orchestrator receives improvement session trigger → reads prior journal (if resume) → enters iteration loop → calls agent-improver (proposal-only) → evaluates candidate using 5-dimension scoring → calls trade-off-detector → appends events to journal via improvement-journal.cjs → updates mutation-coverage.cjs → checks legal-stop gates → emits stop-reason and session-ended event → optionally triggers benchmark-stability.cjs post-session → optionally triggers weight optimizer (separate invocation).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:701:- [ ] Add session-id propagation to improvement_config.json

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2268:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/004-offline-loop-optimizer/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:6663:   164	| REQ-004 | Both loops MUST produce append-only audit journals that capture file reads, searches, evidence extraction, focus/dimension rotation decisions, and convergence-calculation inputs. | Runtime artifacts include a canonical journal file per session, it is append-only, reducer/dashboard flows can summarize it, and stop/recovery analysis can be reproduced from packet artifacts without hidden state. | `CF-010` [SOURCE: `.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/999-agentic-system-upgrade/001-research-agentic-systems/consolidated-research-report.md:168-177`] |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2410:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:30:**Context**: Phase 2 built a coverage graph in `deep-loop-graph.sqlite`. Agent-improver needs similar mutation tracking.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2411:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/decision-record.md:31:**Decision**: Reuse the same SQLite database with `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace. Node kinds: dimension, mutation_type, integration_surface, candidate.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2421:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:24:The sk-improve-agent skill runs bounded improvement loops over agent packets, but it currently lacks the runtime truth infrastructure proven in 042 Phases 1-4 for deep-research and deep-review. This phase ports those contracts into agent-improver: a well-formed stop-reason taxonomy, legal-stop gates, audit journaling, mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory analysis, trade-off detection, optional parallel candidate waves, and scoring weight optimization based on session history.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2422:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:26:**Key Decisions**: emit journal events in the orchestrator (not in the proposal-only agent); reuse the existing coverage graph infrastructure with an improvement-specific namespace; treat dimension trajectory as a first-class convergence signal; keep parallel candidate waves opt-in behind an exploration-breadth gate.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2425:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:69:- Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all session events
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2429:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:77:- Scoring weight optimizer using historical session data
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2434:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:98:| `.opencode/command/improve/agent.md` | Modify | Add resume semantics, session-id propagation, weight optimizer invocation |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2435:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:99:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/improvement-journal.cjs` | Create | Append-only JSONL event emitter for improvement session events |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2438:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:105:| `.opencode/skill/sk-improve-agent/scripts/tests/mutation-coverage.vitest.ts` | Create | Tests for coverage graph read/write and namespace isolation |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2440:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:120:| REQ-AI-001 | Stop-reason taxonomy: `converged`, `promoted`, `rolledBack`, `maxIterationsReached`, `regressionDetected`, `manualStop`, `error` | Every session termination emits exactly one stop-reason matching the taxonomy; no session ends without a logged stop-reason |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2441:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:122:| REQ-AI-003 | Resume/continuation semantics for improvement sessions | A session started with a prior session-id replays journal state and resumes from the last checkpoint without repeating completed iterations |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2442:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:123:| REQ-AI-004 | Audit journal (`improvement-journal.jsonl`) capturing all events (iteration-started, candidate-proposed, candidate-evaluated, promotion-gate-checked, session-ended) | All five event types appear in the journal for every completed session; file is append-only and survives process restart |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2443:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:130:| REQ-AI-006 | Mutation coverage graph tracking explored dimensions, tried mutations, and integration surfaces using `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace | After each iteration the coverage graph is updated; querying it returns accurate explored/remaining sets per dimension |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2444:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:131:| REQ-AI-007 | Dimension trajectory tracking (per-dimension score history across iterations) | For each active session the trajectory file contains a time-ordered score vector per dimension; minimum 3 data points before convergence can be claimed |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2447:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:146:| REQ-AI-012 | Scoring weight optimizer using historical session data to recommend per-dimension weight adjustments | After a configurable number of sessions the optimizer reads the journal history and emits a weight-recommendation report; recommendations do not auto-apply without explicit approval |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2448:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:147:| REQ-AI-013 | Benchmark replay stability measurement confirming consistent scores across identical replays | The benchmark-stability script reports a stability coefficient for each dimension; sessions with coefficient below threshold emit a `stabilityWarning` to the journal |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2449:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:155:- **SC-001**: Every improvement session termination produces a journal entry with exactly one stop-reason from the approved taxonomy.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2451:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:161:- **SC-007**: The scoring weight optimizer produces a recommendation report after the configured session count threshold is reached.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2453:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:174:| Dependency | 042 Phase 2 coverage graph | Coverage graph namespace and API must support `loop_type: "improvement"` | Add namespace field to existing graph writers; verify no breaking change to deep-research/review paths |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2456:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:177:| Risk | Parallel wave complexity overloading LLM context | Parallel waves increase context length per session | Keep parallel waves strictly opt-in; default config disables them |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2466:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:245:| R-002 | Coverage graph namespace collision with deep-research/review paths | Medium | Low | `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace isolation; tested in mutation-coverage.vitest.ts |
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2467:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:256:**As a** developer running an agent improvement session, **I want** every session to produce a structured audit journal, **so that** I can reconstruct exactly what mutations were tried, how they scored, and why the session stopped.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2468:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:259:1. Given a completed improvement session, When I read the journal file, Then it contains at least one event per iteration plus a session-ended event with a stop-reason.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2469:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:260:2. Given a session that terminated due to a regression, When I read the journal, Then the stop-reason is `regressionDetected` and the offending dimension scores are recorded.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2470:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:269:1. Given a session-id from a prior partial run, When I start a new session with that id, Then the orchestrator replays journal state and the iteration counter starts where the prior run left off.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2471:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:270:2. Given a resumed session, When it completes, Then the final journal contains events from both the prior run and the resumed run in chronological order.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2476:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:299:1. Given the configured session count threshold is reached, When the weight optimizer runs, Then it emits a weight-recommendation report showing current vs. recommended weights with supporting evidence.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2478:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:310:2. Given a coefficient below the configured threshold, When the session runs, Then a `stabilityWarning` event appears in the journal.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2479:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/spec.md:317:- Should the improvement journal share the same file path convention as the deep-research/deep-review journals, or use a separate directory under the skill? Recommendation: use a skill-local path (`~/.agent-improver/sessions/{session-id}/`) consistent with the packet-local discipline.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2492:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:45:- [ ] 042 Phase 2 coverage graph API confirmed to support `loop_type` namespace parameter
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2494:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:70:- **improvement-journal.cjs**: Append-only JSONL emitter. Receives structured event objects from the orchestrator and appends them to the session journal file. Enforces the event schema and blocks invalid event types.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2495:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:71:- **mutation-coverage.cjs**: Coverage graph reader/writer. Tracks explored dimensions, tried mutation types per dimension, integration surfaces touched, and exhausted mutation sets. Uses `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace to isolate from deep-research/review paths.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2497:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:73:- **candidate-lineage.cjs**: Builds and queries a directed graph of candidate proposals across parallel wave sessions. Links each node to its parent session-id, wave index, and spawning mutation type.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2499:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:78:Orchestrator receives improvement session trigger → reads prior journal (if resume) → enters iteration loop → calls agent-improver (proposal-only) → evaluates candidate using 5-dimension scoring → calls trade-off-detector → appends events to journal via improvement-journal.cjs → updates mutation-coverage.cjs → checks legal-stop gates → emits stop-reason and session-ended event → optionally triggers benchmark-stability.cjs post-session → optionally triggers weight optimizer (separate invocation).
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2505:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:99:- [ ] Manual integration test: run a session and confirm journal output
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2506:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:103:Add mutation coverage tracking, dimension trajectory, trade-off detection, and exhausted-mutations logging. Reuses the Phase 2 coverage graph infrastructure via namespace isolation.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2507:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:105:- [ ] Create `mutation-coverage.cjs` with `loop_type: "improvement"` namespace (REQ-AI-006)
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2513:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:112:- [ ] Manual integration test: run 3-iteration session and inspect coverage graph
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2515:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:126:Add scoring weight optimizer using historical session data and benchmark replay stability measurement.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2520:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/plan.md:143:| Integration | Orchestrator loop with journal emit, coverage graph update, legal-stop gate | Manual session run + journal inspection |

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2597:/bin/zsh -lc 'for d in 001-runtime-truth-foundation 002-semantic-coverage-graph 003-wave-executor 004-offline-loop-optimizer; do base=.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/$d; echo "===== $d spec anchors ====="; rg -n "stop-reason|legal stop|resume|journal|ledger|coverage graph|loop_type|wave|parallel|optimizer|weight|stability|trade-off|trajectory|convergence|replay" "$base"/{spec.md,plan.md,decision-record.md,implementation-summary.md,checklist.md} 2>/dev/null | head -n 120; echo; done' in /Users/michelkerkmeester/MEGA/Development/Code_Environment/Public
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2611:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/implementation-summary.md:35:Deep research and deep review now have explicit, auditable runtime truth. Every loop stop reports a named reason from a shared taxonomy, every stop decision passes through a legal-stop gate that checks convergence, coverage, and quality together, and every session carries a resume cursor that makes continuation deterministic rather than inferred.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2613:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/implementation-summary.md:43:Sessions now carry `continuedFromRun` semantics that work for both active resume (loop was blocked and continues) and completed-continue (loop finished and a new session extends the prior work). This lineage is explicit in commands, workflows, state contracts, and agent instructions.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2699:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/001-runtime-truth-foundation/plan.md:118:**Why first**: Every later improvement depends on a trusted answer to four questions: why did the loop stop, was STOP legal, where can a session resume, and can the stop decision be replayed from packet-local state. The agent instructions also need to stop fighting the reducer contract before broader runtime work begins.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2721:===== 002-semantic-coverage-graph spec anchors =====
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2722:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:3:description: "Deliver the deep-loop coverage graph substrate by extracting shared graph primitives, adding dedicated storage and MCP tools, and wiring graph-backed convergence into deep research and deep review."
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2723:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:7:  - "semantic coverage graph"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2724:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:9:  - "deep_loop_graph_convergence"
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2725:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:36:This phase is a concrete build plan, not a moonshot placeholder. We already know which graph primitives exist, how they map to coverage semantics, which tables are required, what the MCP tools need to do, and how graph convergence must interact with Phase 001 runtime truth. Research findings also show that reuse is narrower than first estimated, so the plan now makes adaptation work explicit instead of treating it as incidental. The sequence is:
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/005-agent-improver-deep-loop-alignment/scratch/codex-gpt54-deep-research.md:2726:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/042-sk-deep-research-review-improvement-2/002-semantic-coverage-graph/plan.md:42:5.
